revision.text: {{Short description|Wikimedia project page for citation}} {{pp-semi-indef|small=yes}} {{redirect|Wikipedia:Ref tools|referencing toolbar that is part of the default Wikipedia editor|Wikipedia:RefToolbar}} {{Wikipedia how to|WP:CITETOOL}} The following tools can help you assemble a citation from limited information, with limited effort, although you should check their output for accuracy. These are tools with a variety of interfaces that provide a complete formatted reference based on a few initial details. == General == * [[User:ProveIt GT|ProveIt]] provides a graphical interface for editing, adding, and citing references. It may be enabled per the documentation * [[Wikipedia:RefToolbar|RefToolbar]] is part of the editing tool bar == Generators == {{shortcut|WP:CITEGENERATORS}} {{See also|Wikipedia:Citing sources#Programming tools}} {{Editor tools}} * '''[http://sumsearch.org/cite/ Biomedical citation maker]''' by [[User:Badgettrg]]. Converts a PubMed ID (PMID), DOI, PMCID, NCT to {{tl|cite journal}} or simple wikiformatting for all journals indexed by PubMed. Bookmarklet is available. Adds links to [https://web.archive.org/web/20161022143433/http://annals.org/aim/journal-club ACP Journal Club] and [http://ebm.bmj.com/ Evidence-Based Medicine] comments if present. *'''[https://citationhunt.toolforge.org/ Citation Hunt]''': A tool for browsing snippets of Wikipedia articles that [[Template:cn|lack citations]]. * '''[https://citer.toolforge.org/ Citer]''': Converts a URL, DOI, ISBN, PMID, PMCID, [[:en:OCLC|OCLC]], or [[:en:Google Books|Google Books]] URL into a citation and [[Help:Shortened footnotes|shortened footnote]]. It also can generate citations for [https://github.com/5j9/citer#what-does-it-do certain major news websites] (e.g., [[The New York Times]]) and the [[:en:Wayback Machine|Wayback Machine]]. * '''[[Wikipedia:TWL/Citoid|Citoid]]''': A tool built into both [[wikipedia:VisualEditor|Visual Editor]] and source editor that attempts to build a full citation based on a URL. See [[Wikipedia:VisualEditor/User guide#Using Citoid|user guide]]. * '''[https://citation-template-filling.toolforge.org/cgi-bin/index.cgi Diberri Template builder]''': Converts URL, DrugBank ID, HGNC ID, PubMed ID, PubMed Central ID or PubChem ID to full citation. * '''[https://makeref.toolforge.org MakeRef]''': A form for creating various {{cite xxx}} templates. * '''[[WP:OABOT|OABOT]]''', a tool that finds open-access links for citations * '''[[m:Web2Cit|Web2Cit]]''': An automatic citation generator for web sources, meant to complement citation results by Citoid for which no valid ''translators'' exist. Web2Cit translators are community controlled. It runs [https://web2cit.toolforge.org/ its own server on toolforge]. * '''[https://www.mediawiki.org/wiki/Microsoft_Word_Macros#Wikipedia_AutoReferencer Wikipedia AutoReferencer]''', Microsoft word tool to convert embedded links to wikitext * '''[https://addons.mozilla.org/en/firefox/addon/wikipedia-references-creato/ Wikipedia-References-Creator]''': A Firefox addon for creation of references (output changeable). * [[User:Odie5533/SnipManager|SnipManager]] adds a ribbon interface to add citations * [[User:Cacycle/wikEd|wikEd]] * The [[Wikipedia:VisualEditor|visual editor]] helps users format, insert, and edit sources by simply providing a DOI, URL, ISBN etc., see [[WP:REFVISUAL]]. * The citation generation tool of the Visual Editor ([[WP:REFVISUAL]]) can also be used when editing the article source, for users who have enabled the [https://www.mediawiki.org/wiki/2017_wikitext_editor 2017 wikitext editor] in their preferences. * [[User:Badgettrg]], [https://sumsearch.org/cite/ Biomedical citation maker]. uses Pubmed ID (PMID) or DOI or PMCID or NCT. Adds links to [https://annals.org/aim/journal-club ACP Journal Club] and [https://ebm.bmj.com/ Evidence-Based Medicine] comments if present. * [[Template:Ref info]], which can aid evaluating what kind of citation style was used to write the article * Based on [[Wikipedia:The Wikipedia Library/Citoid|Citoid]]: ** [[Wikipedia:VisualEditor/User guide#Using standard cite templates|Cite templates in Visual Editor]] ** [[User:Salix alba/Citoid]] a client for the [[mw:citoid]] server which generates {{cs1}} templates from urls. * Hosted on tools.wmflabs.org: ** [[Wikipedia:refToolbar 2.0]], used in the Source Editor ** [[User:Citation bot|Citation bot]] ** [https://tools.wmflabs.org/yadkard/ Yadkard]: A web-based tool for generating [[Help:Shortened footnotes|shortened footnotes]] and citation using Google Books URLs, DOI or ISBN. Also supports some news websites. ** [https://tools.wmflabs.org/citation-template-filling/cgi-bin/index.cgi?ddb=&type=pubmed_id&id=&add_param_space=1&add_ref_tag=1&full_journal_title=1 Wikipedia template filling] – generates [[Vancouver style]] citations from PMIDs ([[:en:PubMed|PubMed]] IDs). ===Templates=== * [https://citations.toolforge.org/ Citation Bot] ([[User:Citation bot|more info]]) – Partial citations must either contain a DOI, PMID, PMC, ISBN, [[:en:S2CID|S2CID]] or enough fields to be uniquely found; the bot will also fix formatting errors * [[User:Rjwilmsi/CiteCompletion|CiteCompletion]] for [[WP:AWB|AutoWikiBrowser]] – A custom module for [[WP:AWB|AWB]] to complete citations of common English-language news sites * [[Template:Find sources|Find sources]] – provides links to customized searches for reliable sources in news newspapers books scholar JSTOR free images and the like. Points to a variety of templates for use in talk pages, on the [[Wikipedia:Articles for deletion|AfD]] page etc., and for a variety of types of articles and situations. * [[User:PleaseStand/References segregator|References segregator]] ([[:en:JavaScript|JavaScript]]) – Shows references in a separate edit window for easy editing; can also convert references to [[Help:Footnotes#List-defined references|list-defined references]] format * [[Wikipedia:RefToolbar|refToolbar]] ([[:en:JavaScript|JavaScript]]) – Allows you to format a reference during editing when you already have all the data * [[User:Smith609/Scopus|Scopus search add-on]] – Find a reference on [[Scopus]], then with one click it's formatted ready for use in an article * [[User:Odie5533/SnipManager|SnipManager]] ([[:en:JavaScript|JavaScript]]) – Adds a Ribbon menu above the edit form with templates (including citations) and the ability to preview citations * [[User:CitationTool]] – Semi-bot for finding citation errors and fixing them === Bookmarklets === * [[Wikipedia:Cite4Wiki|Cite4Wiki]], an [[:en:XUL|XUL]]-based add-on for [[:en:Pale Moon|Pale Moon]] to generate {{tl|cite web}} and {{tl|cite news}} for the browser's current page. * [[User:Bazzargh/citemark|citemark]] ([[:en:JavaScript|JavaScript]]) – A [[:en:Bookmarklet|Bookmarklet]] to help create {{tl|cite web}} templates; see the [[User:Bazzargh/citemark|developer's page]] for details * '''[[User:Ark25/RefScript|RefScript]]''': A bookmarklet that generates references with a single click. Works with a few news websites (BBC, Daily Mirror, Daily Telegraph, Huffington Post, Huffington Post Canada, New York Times, Washington Post, Boston Globe, Times of India, Financial Times, The Economist, Business Week, Ars Technica, TG Daily) and it can learn any other newspaper or website. * '''[[User:V111P/js/WebRef|WebRef]]''': A bookmarklet automating the filling of the {{tl|cite web}} template. * [[User:Ark25/RefScript]], a JavaScript bookmarklet – creates references in one click, works for many newspapers * [[User:V111P/js/WebRef]], a script or bookmarklet automating the filling of the {{tl|cite web}} template. You use the script on the page you want to cite. === Desktop software === * [[Wikipedia:Wikicite|Wikicite]] is a free program that helps editors to create citations for their Wikipedia contributions using [[Wikipedia:citation templates|citation templates]]. It is written in [[Visual Basic .NET]], making it suitable only for users with the [[:en:.NET Framework|.NET Framework]] installed on Windows, or, for other platforms, the [[:en:Mono (software)|Mono]] alternative framework. Wikicite and its source code is freely available; see the [[Wikipedia:Wikicite|developer's page]] for further details. ===Reference management software=== Reference management software can output formatted citations in several styles, including Wikipedia citation templates. * [[:en:Comparison of reference management software|Comparison of reference management software]] – side-by-side comparison of various [[:en:Reference management software|Reference management software]] * [[Wikipedia:Citing sources with Zotero]] – essay on using [[Zotero]] to quickly add citations to articles. * [[User:Nathanstrait/Wikipedia BibDesk Export Template|BibDesk Export Template]] – An export template for formatting [[Wikipedia:Citation templates|Wikipedia citation templates]] in [[:en:BibDesk|BibDesk]], which is a free [[:en:Open-source|Open-source]] [[:en:BibTeX|BibTeX]]-based [[:en:Reference management software|Reference management software]] application for [[:en:MacOS|MacOS]] ===User scripts=== * [[User:TheDJ/Folded references|Folded references]] collapses the references of a page * [[User:Blue-Haired Lawyer/Footnote popups|Footnote popups]] Displays a small popup box of a footnote when you hover the cursor above a footnote link * [[User:Ucucha/HarvErrors|HarvErrors]] shows errors when using Harvard templates * [[Template:Listref|ListrefErrors]] shows errors when using {{tl|listref}} * [[User:PleaseStand/References segregator|PleaseStand/References segregator]] places references into a separate edit box * [[User:Smith609/endnote.js|Reference exporter]] * [[User:Cumbril/Reference Organizer|Reference Organizer]] presents all references in graphical user interface, where you can choose whether the references should be defined in the body of article or in the reference list template(s) ([[Help:List-defined references|list-defined format]]). You can also sort the references in various ways (and optionally keep the sort order), and rename the references. * [[User:Anomie/reftooltip.js|RefTooltip]] sets the tooltip for references to the text of the reference. * [[User:Lingzhi2/reviewsourcecheck|ReviewSourceCheck]] (different versions for different preferences) flags 16 types of errors in the references and/or notes, when using Harv templates. * [[User:Ohconfucius/script/Sources|Sources]] Formats names of newspapers within citation templates * [[User:Anomie/ajaxpreview.js]] adds a preview button that will show references when editing a section * [[User:BrandonXLF/Autoref]] replaces the reference button in the editing toolbar in the 2010 wikitext editor with a button that allows the use of Citoid to insert an auto generated reference * [[User:BrandonXLF/Citoid]] generates a reference using the Citoid server. Designed for being used inside user scripts. * [[user:js/ajaxPreview]] adds a preview button that will show references when editing a section * [[User:Salix alba/Citoid]] Generates citation templates using the Citoid server. Standalone javascript which can be used outside of Visual Editor. == Tools to improve existing citations == * [[Wikipedia:Citation expander|Citation expander]] automatically adds missing data to citations using citation templates, and makes corrections to their formatting * [[Wikipedia:ReFill|reFill]] expands bare url references ==Duplicate reference finders== Finding duplicate references by examining reference lists is difficult. There are some tools that can help: * [[Wikipedia:AutoWikiBrowser|AutoWikiBrowser]] (AWB) will identify and (usually) correct ''exact duplicates'' between <ref>...</ref> tags. See [[Wikipedia:HTADC|the documentation]]. * [https://www.convertcsv.com/url-extractor.htm URL Extractor For Web Pages and Text] can identify Web citations with the ''exact same URL'' but otherwise possibly different. Most differences are not significant, but sometimes different page numbers from the same URL are cited. Occasionally references to the same Web page might be followed by different non-significant tracking parameters (?utm ..., #ixzz...), and will not be listed as duplicates. ** Step 1: click "Enter URL", enter (paste) the URL of the Wikipedia article and click "Load", ** Step 2: tick "Only Display duplicate URL addresses" (which unticks "Remove duplicate addresses") ***Optional: Tick the radio button "Do not show", tick the box at the beginning of its line, and enter (paste) into the box web.archive.org,wikipedia,wikimedia,wikiquote,wikidata,worldcat ** Step 3: Click Extract. ** Then the duplicates will be listed, and must be manually merged. There will often be false positives; web.archive.org URLs, in particular, are a nuisance as they contain the original URLs, which show as duplicates. The optional part of Step 2 eliminates the archive URLs, but unfortunately the list of duplicates includes the archived pages. The wiki* URLs are less of a problem as they can just be ignored. == Libraries for developers == * [[User:Richiez]] wrote {{oldid2|536234043|Scripts to do automatic PMID/ISBN referencing|tools to automatically handle citations}} for a whole article at a time. These were marked as [[Special:Diff/705834247|obsolete in 2016]]. They convert occurrences of {{pmid|XXXXXXXX}} ([[:en:PubMed|PubMed]]) or {{isbn|XXXXXXXXXX}} to properly formatted footnote or Harvard-style references. Written in [[Ruby (programming language)|Ruby]] and requires a working installation with basic libraries. * [https://github.com/lindenb/xslt-sandbox/blob/master/stylesheets/bio/ncbi/pubmed2wikipedia.xsl pubmed2wikipedia.xsl] an [[:en:Extensible Stylesheet Language|XSL]] stylesheet transforming the XML output of [[:en:PubMed|PubMed]] to Wikipedia refs. ==Documentation== These templates can be used in documentation: '''Typing aids''' * {{tl|cite *}}: shortcut for {{tl|Citation/core}} * {{tl|cite xxx}}: shortcut for [[Wikipedia:Citation templates]] * {{tl|cite.php}}: shortcut for [[mw:Extension:Cite]] * {{tl|cs1}}: shortcut for [[Help: Citation Style 1]] * {{tl|cs2}}: shortcut for [[Help: Citation Style 2]] * {{tl|fnote}}: shortcut for [[Help:Footnotes]] * {{tl|ldr}}: shortcut for [[Help:Footnotes#WP:LDR|List-defined references]] * {{tl|paren}}: shortcut for [[Wikipedia:Parenthetical referencing]] * {{tl|sfnote}}: shortcut for [[Help:Shortened footnotes]] * {{tl|vcite}}: shortcut for [[Help:Citation Style Vancouver]] '''Navboxes''' * {{tl|Citation Style 1}}: navbox for {{cs1}} templates * {{tl|Wikipedia referencing}}: navbox for referencing help pages '''Debug''' * {{tl|Cite compare}}: a tool for comparing the output of the various versions of a {{cs1}} template '''Documentation''' * {{tl|Citation Style documentation}}: modular documentation for {{cs1}} and {{cs2}} * {{tl|Harvard citation documentation}}: documentation for Harvard citations * {{tl|Markup}}: show markup and rendered output side by side * {{tl|Refname rules}}: rules for names for footnotes and groups '''Replication''' * {{tl|Dummy backlink}}: replicate footnote backlinks * {{tl|Dummy ref}}: replicate footnote in-text cite * {{tl|Fake heading}}: replicate headings * {{tl|Fake notes and references}}: replicate Notes and References headings {{Wikipedia referencing}} {{Wikipedia technical help|collapsed}} [[Category:Wikipedia tools]]

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki

Written in [[Ruby (programming language)|Ruby]] and requires a working installation with basic libraries. * [https://github.com/lindenb/xslt-sandbox/blob/master/stylesheets/bio/ncbi/pubmed2wikipedia.xsl pubmed2wikipedia.xsl] an [[:en:Extensible Stylesheet Language|XSL]] stylesheet transforming the XML output of [[:en:PubMed|PubMed]] to Wikipedia refs. ==Documentation== These templates can be used in documentation: '''Typing aids''' * {{tl|cite *}}: shortcut for {{tl|Citation/core}} * {{tl|cite xxx}}: shortcut for [[Wikipedia:Citation templates]] * {{tl|cite.php}}: shortcut for [[mw:Extension:Cite]] * {{tl|cs1}}: shortcut for [[Help: Citation Style 1]] * {{tl|cs2}}: shortcut for [[Help: Citation Style 2]] * {{tl|fnote}}: shortcut for [[Help:Footnotes]] * {{tl|ldr}}: shortcut for [[Help:Footnotes#WP:LDR|List-defined references]] * {{tl|paren}}: shortcut for [[Wikipedia:Parenthetical referencing]] * {{tl|sfnote}}: shortcut for [[Help:Shortened footnotes]] * {{tl|vcite}}: shortcut for [[Help:Citation Style Vancouver]] '''Navboxes''' * {{tl|Citation Style 1}}: navbox for {{cs1}} templates * {{tl|Wikipedia referencing}}: navbox for referencing help pages '''Debug''' * {{tl|Cite compare}}: a tool for comparing the output of the various versions of a {{cs1}} template '''Documentation''' * {{tl|Citation Style documentation}}: modular documentation for {{cs1}} and {{cs2}} * {{tl|Harvard citation documentation}}: documentation for Harvard citations * {{tl|Markup}}: show markup and rendered output side by side * {{tl|Refname rules}}: rules for names for footnotes and groups '''Replication''' * {{tl|Dummy backlink}}: replicate footnote backlinks * {{tl|Dummy ref}}: replicate footnote in-text cite * {{tl|Fake heading}}: replicate headings * {{tl|Fake notes and references}}: replicate Notes and References headings {{Wikipedia referencing}} {{Wikipedia technical help|collapsed}} [[Category:Wikipedia tools]]

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text: {{Short description|Wikimedia project page for citation}} {{pp-semi-indef|small=yes}} {{redirect|Wikipedia:Ref tools|referencing toolbar that is part of the default Wikipedia editor|Wikipedia:RefToolbar}} {{Wikipedia how to|WP:CITETOOL}} The following tools can help you assemble a citation from limited information, with limited effort, although you should check their output for accuracy. These are tools with a variety of interfaces that provide a complete formatted reference based on a few initial details. == General == * [[User:ProveIt GT|ProveIt]] provides a graphical interface for editing, adding, and citing references. It may be enabled per the documentation * [[Wikipedia:RefToolbar|RefToolbar]] is part of the editing tool bar == Generators == {{shortcut|WP:CITEGENERATORS}} {{See also|Wikipedia:Citing sources#Programming tools}} {{Editor tools}} * '''[http://sumsearch.org/cite/ Biomedical citation maker]''' by [[User:Badgettrg]]. Converts a PubMed ID (PMID), DOI, PMCID, NCT to {{tl|cite journal}} or simple wikiformatting for all journals indexed by PubMed. Bookmarklet is available. Adds links to [https://web.archive.org/web/20161022143433/http://annals.org/aim/journal-club ACP Journal Club] and [http://ebm.bmj.com/ Evidence-Based Medicine] comments if present. *'''[https://citationhunt.toolforge.org/ Citation Hunt]''': A tool for browsing snippets of Wikipedia articles that [[Template:cn|lack citations]]. * '''[https://citer.toolforge.org/ Citer]''': Converts a URL, DOI, ISBN, PMID, PMCID, [[:en:OCLC|OCLC]], or [[:en:Google Books|Google Books]] URL into a citation and [[Help:Shortened footnotes|shortened footnote]]. It also can generate citations for [https://github.com/5j9/citer#what-does-it-do certain major news websites] (e.g., [[The New York Times]]) and the [[:en:Wayback Machine|Wayback Machine]]. * '''[[Wikipedia:TWL/Citoid|Citoid]]''': A tool built into both [[wikipedia:VisualEditor|Visual Editor]] and source editor that attempts to build a full citation based on a URL. See [[Wikipedia:VisualEditor/User guide#Using Citoid|user guide]]. * '''[https://citation-template-filling.toolforge.org/cgi-bin/index.cgi Diberri Template builder]''': Converts URL, DrugBank ID, HGNC ID, PubMed ID, PubMed Central ID or PubChem ID to full citation. * '''[https://makeref.toolforge.org MakeRef]''': A form for creating various {{cite xxx}} templates. * '''[[WP:OABOT|OABOT]]''', a tool that finds open-access links for citations * '''[[m:Web2Cit|Web2Cit]]''': An automatic citation generator for web sources, meant to complement citation results by Citoid for which no valid ''translators'' exist. Web2Cit translators are community controlled. It runs [https://web2cit.toolforge.org/ its own server on toolforge]. * '''[https://www.mediawiki.org/wiki/Microsoft_Word_Macros#Wikipedia_AutoReferencer Wikipedia AutoReferencer]''', Microsoft word tool to convert embedded links to wikitext * '''[https://addons.mozilla.org/en/firefox/addon/wikipedia-references-creato/ Wikipedia-References-Creator]''': A Firefox addon for creation of references (output changeable). * [[User:Odie5533/SnipManager|SnipManager]] adds a ribbon interface to add citations * [[User:Cacycle/wikEd|wikEd]] * The [[Wikipedia:VisualEditor|visual editor]] helps users format, insert, and edit sources by simply providing a DOI, URL, ISBN etc., see [[WP:REFVISUAL]]. * The citation generation tool of the Visual Editor ([[WP:REFVISUAL]]) can also be used when editing the article source, for users who have enabled the [https://www.mediawiki.org/wiki/2017_wikitext_editor 2017 wikitext editor] in their preferences. * [[User:Badgettrg]], [https://sumsearch.org/cite/ Biomedical citation maker]. uses Pubmed ID (PMID) or DOI or PMCID or NCT. Adds links to [https://annals.org/aim/journal-club ACP Journal Club] and [https://ebm.bmj.com/ Evidence-Based Medicine] comments if present. * [[Template:Ref info]], which can aid evaluating what kind of citation style was used to write the article * Based on [[Wikipedia:The Wikipedia Library/Citoid|Citoid]]: ** [[Wikipedia:VisualEditor/User guide#Using standard cite templates|Cite templates in Visual Editor]] ** [[User:Salix alba/Citoid]] a client for the [[mw:citoid]] server which generates {{cs1}} templates from urls. * Hosted on tools.wmflabs.org: ** [[Wikipedia:refToolbar 2.0]], used in the Source Editor ** [[User:Citation bot|Citation bot]] ** [https://tools.wmflabs.org/yadkard/ Yadkard]: A web-based tool for generating [[Help:Shortened footnotes|shortened footnotes]] and citation using Google Books URLs, DOI or ISBN. Also supports some news websites. ** [https://tools.wmflabs.org/citation-template-filling/cgi-bin/index.cgi?ddb=&type=pubmed_id&id=&add_param_space=1&add_ref_tag=1&full_journal_title=1 Wikipedia template filling] – generates [[Vancouver style]] citations from PMIDs ([[:en:PubMed|PubMed]] IDs). ===Templates=== * [https://citations.toolforge.org/ Citation Bot] ([[User:Citation bot|more info]]) – Partial citations must either contain a DOI, PMID, PMC, ISBN, [[:en:S2CID|S2CID]] or enough fields to be uniquely found; the bot will also fix formatting errors * [[User:Rjwilmsi/CiteCompletion|CiteCompletion]] for [[WP:AWB|AutoWikiBrowser]] – A custom module for [[WP:AWB|AWB]] to complete citations of common English-language news sites * [[Template:Find sources|Find sources]] – provides links to customized searches for reliable sources in news newspapers books scholar JSTOR free images and the like. Points to a variety of templates for use in talk pages, on the [[Wikipedia:Articles for deletion|AfD]] page etc., and for a variety of types of articles and situations. * [[User:PleaseStand/References segregator|References segregator]] ([[:en:JavaScript|JavaScript]]) – Shows references in a separate edit window for easy editing; can also convert references to [[Help:Footnotes#List-defined references|list-defined references]] format * [[Wikipedia:RefToolbar|refToolbar]] ([[:en:JavaScript|JavaScript]]) – Allows you to format a reference during editing when you already have all the data * [[User:Smith609/Scopus|Scopus search add-on]] – Find a reference on [[Scopus]], then with one click it's formatted ready for use in an article * [[User:Odie5533/SnipManager|SnipManager]] ([[:en:JavaScript|JavaScript]]) – Adds a Ribbon menu above the edit form with templates (including citations) and the ability to preview citations * [[User:CitationTool]] – Semi-bot for finding citation errors and fixing them === Bookmarklets === * [[Wikipedia:Cite4Wiki|Cite4Wiki]], an [[:en:XUL|XUL]]-based add-on for [[:en:Pale Moon|Pale Moon]] to generate {{tl|cite web}} and {{tl|cite news}} for the browser's current page. * [[User:Bazzargh/citemark|citemark]] ([[:en:JavaScript|JavaScript]]) – A [[:en:Bookmarklet|Bookmarklet]] to help create {{tl|cite web}} templates; see the [[User:Bazzargh/citemark|developer's page]] for details * '''[[User:Ark25/RefScript|RefScript]]''': A bookmarklet that generates references with a single click. Works with a few news websites (BBC, Daily Mirror, Daily Telegraph, Huffington Post, Huffington Post Canada, New York Times, Washington Post, Boston Globe, Times of India, Financial Times, The Economist, Business Week, Ars Technica, TG Daily) and it can learn any other newspaper or website. * '''[[User:V111P/js/WebRef|WebRef]]''': A bookmarklet automating the filling of the {{tl|cite web}} template. * [[User:Ark25/RefScript]], a JavaScript bookmarklet – creates references in one click, works for many newspapers * [[User:V111P/js/WebRef]], a script or bookmarklet automating the filling of the {{tl|cite web}} template. You use the script on the page you want to cite. === Desktop software === * [[Wikipedia:Wikicite|Wikicite]] is a free program that helps editors to create citations for their Wikipedia contributions using [[Wikipedia:citation templates|citation templates]]. It is written in [[Visual Basic .NET]], making it suitable only for users with the [[:en:.NET Framework|.NET Framework]] installed on Windows, or, for other platforms, the [[:en:Mono (software)|Mono]] alternative framework. Wikicite and its source code is freely available; see the [[Wikipedia:Wikicite|developer's page]] for further details. ===Reference management software=== Reference management software can output formatted citations in several styles, including Wikipedia citation templates. * [[:en:Comparison of reference management software|Comparison of reference management software]] – side-by-side comparison of various [[:en:Reference management software|Reference management software]] * [[Wikipedia:Citing sources with Zotero]] – essay on using [[:en:Zotero|Zotero]] to quickly add citations to articles. * [[User:Nathanstrait/Wikipedia BibDesk Export Template|BibDesk Export Template]] – An export template for formatting [[Wikipedia:Citation templates|Wikipedia citation templates]] in [[:en:BibDesk|BibDesk]], which is a free [[:en:Open-source|Open-source]] [[:en:BibTeX|BibTeX]]-based [[:en:Reference management software|Reference management software]] application for [[:en:MacOS|MacOS]] ===User scripts=== * [[User:TheDJ/Folded references|Folded references]] collapses the references of a page * [[User:Blue-Haired Lawyer/Footnote popups|Footnote popups]] Displays a small popup box of a footnote when you hover the cursor above a footnote link * [[User:Ucucha/HarvErrors|HarvErrors]] shows errors when using Harvard templates * [[Template:Listref|ListrefErrors]] shows errors when using {{tl|listref}} * [[User:PleaseStand/References segregator|PleaseStand/References segregator]] places references into a separate edit box * [[User:Smith609/endnote.js|Reference exporter]] * [[User:Cumbril/Reference Organizer|Reference Organizer]] presents all references in graphical user interface, where you can choose whether the references should be defined in the body of article or in the reference list template(s) ([[Help:List-defined references|list-defined format]]). You can also sort the references in various ways (and optionally keep the sort order), and rename the references. * [[User:Anomie/reftooltip.js|RefTooltip]] sets the tooltip for references to the text of the reference. * [[User:Lingzhi2/reviewsourcecheck|ReviewSourceCheck]] (different versions for different preferences) flags 16 types of errors in the references and/or notes, when using Harv templates. * [[User:Ohconfucius/script/Sources|Sources]] Formats names of newspapers within citation templates * [[User:Anomie/ajaxpreview.js]] adds a preview button that will show references when editing a section * [[User:BrandonXLF/Autoref]] replaces the reference button in the editing toolbar in the 2010 wikitext editor with a button that allows the use of Citoid to insert an auto generated reference * [[User:BrandonXLF/Citoid]] generates a reference using the Citoid server. Designed for being used inside user scripts. * [[user:js/ajaxPreview]] adds a preview button that will show references when editing a section * [[User:Salix alba/Citoid]] Generates citation templates using the Citoid server. Standalone javascript which can be used outside of Visual Editor. == Tools to improve existing citations == * [[Wikipedia:Citation expander|Citation expander]] automatically adds missing data to citations using citation templates, and makes corrections to their formatting * [[Wikipedia:ReFill|reFill]] expands bare url references ==Duplicate reference finders== Finding duplicate references by examining reference lists is difficult. There are some tools that can help: * [[Wikipedia:AutoWikiBrowser|AutoWikiBrowser]] (AWB) will identify and (usually) correct ''exact duplicates'' between <ref>...</ref> tags. See [[Wikipedia:HTADC|the documentation]]. * [https://www.convertcsv.com/url-extractor.htm URL Extractor For Web Pages and Text] can identify Web citations with the ''exact same URL'' but otherwise possibly different. Most differences are not significant, but sometimes different page numbers from the same URL are cited. Occasionally references to the same Web page might be followed by different non-significant tracking parameters (?utm ..., #ixzz...), and will not be listed as duplicates. ** Step 1: click "Enter URL", enter (paste) the URL of the Wikipedia article and click "Load", ** Step 2: tick "Only Display duplicate URL addresses" (which unticks "Remove duplicate addresses") ***Optional: Tick the radio button "Do not show", tick the box at the beginning of its line, and enter (paste) into the box web.archive.org,wikipedia,wikimedia,wikiquote,wikidata,worldcat ** Step 3: Click Extract. ** Then the duplicates will be listed, and must be manually merged. There will often be false positives; web.archive.org URLs, in particular, are a nuisance as they contain the original URLs, which show as duplicates. The optional part of Step 2 eliminates the archive URLs, but unfortunately the list of duplicates includes the archived pages. The wiki* URLs are less of a problem as they can just be ignored. == Libraries for developers == * [[User:Richiez]] wrote {{oldid2|536234043|Scripts to do automatic PMID/ISBN referencing|tools to automatically handle citations}} for a whole article at a time. These were marked as [[Special:Diff/705834247|obsolete in 2016]]. They convert occurrences of {{pmid|XXXXXXXX}} ([[:en:PubMed|PubMed]]) or {{isbn|XXXXXXXXXX}} to properly formatted footnote or Harvard-style references. Written in [[:en:Ruby (programming language)|Ruby]] and requires a working installation with basic libraries. * [https://github.com/lindenb/xslt-sandbox/blob/master/stylesheets/bio/ncbi/pubmed2wikipedia.xsl pubmed2wikipedia.xsl] an [[:en:Extensible Stylesheet Language|XSL]] stylesheet transforming the XML output of [[:en:PubMed|PubMed]] to Wikipedia refs. ==Documentation== These templates can be used in documentation: '''Typing aids''' * {{tl|cite *}}: shortcut for {{tl|Citation/core}} * {{tl|cite xxx}}: shortcut for [[Wikipedia:Citation templates]] * {{tl|cite.php}}: shortcut for [[mw:Extension:Cite]] * {{tl|cs1}}: shortcut for [[Help: Citation Style 1]] * {{tl|cs2}}: shortcut for [[Help: Citation Style 2]] * {{tl|fnote}}: shortcut for [[Help:Footnotes]] * {{tl|ldr}}: shortcut for [[Help:Footnotes#WP:LDR|List-defined references]] * {{tl|paren}}: shortcut for [[Wikipedia:Parenthetical referencing]] * {{tl|sfnote}}: shortcut for [[Help:Shortened footnotes]] * {{tl|vcite}}: shortcut for [[Help:Citation Style Vancouver]] '''Navboxes''' * {{tl|Citation Style 1}}: navbox for {{cs1}} templates * {{tl|Wikipedia referencing}}: navbox for referencing help pages '''Debug''' * {{tl|Cite compare}}: a tool for comparing the output of the various versions of a {{cs1}} template '''Documentation''' * {{tl|Citation Style documentation}}: modular documentation for {{cs1}} and {{cs2}} * {{tl|Harvard citation documentation}}: documentation for Harvard citations * {{tl|Markup}}: show markup and rendered output side by side * {{tl|Refname rules}}: rules for names for footnotes and groups '''Replication''' * {{tl|Dummy backlink}}: replicate footnote backlinks * {{tl|Dummy ref}}: replicate footnote in-text cite * {{tl|Fake heading}}: replicate headings * {{tl|Fake notes and references}}: replicate Notes and References headings {{Wikipedia referencing}} {{Wikipedia technical help|collapsed}} [[Category:Wikipedia tools]]

These were marked as [[Special:Diff/705834247|obsolete in 2016]]. They convert occurrences of {{pmid|XXXXXXXX}} ([[:en:PubMed|PubMed]]) or {{isbn|XXXXXXXXXX}} to properly formatted footnote or Harvard-style references. Written in [[:en:Ruby (programming language)|Ruby]] and requires a working installation with basic libraries. * [https://github.com/lindenb/xslt-sandbox/blob/master/stylesheets/bio/ncbi/pubmed2wikipedia.xsl pubmed2wikipedia.xsl] an [[:en:Extensible Stylesheet Language|XSL]] stylesheet transforming the XML output of [[:en:PubMed|PubMed]] to Wikipedia refs. ==Documentation== These templates can be used in documentation: '''Typing aids''' * {{tl|cite *}}: shortcut for {{tl|Citation/core}} * {{tl|cite xxx}}: shortcut for [[Wikipedia:Citation templates]] * {{tl|cite.php}}: shortcut for [[mw:Extension:Cite]] * {{tl|cs1}}: shortcut for [[Help: Citation Style 1]] * {{tl|cs2}}: shortcut for [[Help: Citation Style 2]] * {{tl|fnote}}: shortcut for [[Help:Footnotes]] * {{tl|ldr}}: shortcut for [[Help:Footnotes#WP:LDR|List-defined references]] * {{tl|paren}}: shortcut for [[Wikipedia:Parenthetical referencing]] * {{tl|sfnote}}: shortcut for [[Help:Shortened footnotes]] * {{tl|vcite}}: shortcut for [[Help:Citation Style Vancouver]] '''Navboxes''' * {{tl|Citation Style 1}}: navbox for {{cs1}} templates * {{tl|Wikipedia referencing}}: navbox for referencing help pages '''Debug''' * {{tl|Cite compare}}: a tool for comparing the output of the various versions of a {{cs1}} template '''Documentation''' * {{tl|Citation Style documentation}}: modular documentation for {{cs1}} and {{cs2}} * {{tl|Harvard citation documentation}}: documentation for Harvard citations * {{tl|Markup}}: show markup and rendered output side by side * {{tl|Refname rules}}: rules for names for footnotes and groups '''Replication''' * {{tl|Dummy backlink}}: replicate footnote backlinks * {{tl|Dummy ref}}: replicate footnote in-text cite * {{tl|Fake heading}}: replicate headings * {{tl|Fake notes and references}}: replicate Notes and References headings {{Wikipedia referencing}} {{Wikipedia technical help|collapsed}} [[Category:Wikipedia tools]]

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text: {{Short description|Wikimedia project page for citation}} {{pp-semi-indef|small=yes}} {{redirect|Wikipedia:Ref tools|referencing toolbar that is part of the default Wikipedia editor|Wikipedia:RefToolbar}} {{Wikipedia how to|WP:CITETOOL}} The following tools can help you assemble a citation from limited information, with limited effort, although you should check their output for accuracy. These are tools with a variety of interfaces that provide a complete formatted reference based on a few initial details. == General == * [[User:ProveIt GT|ProveIt]] provides a graphical interface for editing, adding, and citing references. It may be enabled per the documentation * [[Wikipedia:RefToolbar|RefToolbar]] is part of the editing tool bar == Generators == {{shortcut|WP:CITEGENERATORS}} {{See also|Wikipedia:Citing sources#Programming tools}} {{Editor tools}} * '''[http://sumsearch.org/cite/ Biomedical citation maker]''' by [[User:Badgettrg]]. Converts a PubMed ID (PMID), DOI, PMCID, NCT to {{tl|cite journal}} or simple wikiformatting for all journals indexed by PubMed. Bookmarklet is available. Adds links to [https://web.archive.org/web/20161022143433/http://annals.org/aim/journal-club ACP Journal Club] and [http://ebm.bmj.com/ Evidence-Based Medicine] comments if present. *'''[https://citationhunt.toolforge.org/ Citation Hunt]''': A tool for browsing snippets of Wikipedia articles that [[Template:cn|lack citations]]. * '''[https://citer.toolforge.org/ Citer]''': Converts a URL, DOI, ISBN, PMID, PMCID, [[:en:OCLC|OCLC]], or [[:en:Google Books|Google Books]] URL into a citation and [[Help:Shortened footnotes|shortened footnote]]. It also can generate citations for [https://github.com/5j9/citer#what-does-it-do certain major news websites] (e.g., [[The New York Times]]) and the [[:en:Wayback Machine|Wayback Machine]]. * '''[[Wikipedia:TWL/Citoid|Citoid]]''': A tool built into both [[wikipedia:VisualEditor|Visual Editor]] and source editor that attempts to build a full citation based on a URL. See [[Wikipedia:VisualEditor/User guide#Using Citoid|user guide]]. * '''[https://citation-template-filling.toolforge.org/cgi-bin/index.cgi Diberri Template builder]''': Converts URL, DrugBank ID, HGNC ID, PubMed ID, PubMed Central ID or PubChem ID to full citation. * '''[https://makeref.toolforge.org MakeRef]''': A form for creating various {{cite xxx}} templates. * '''[[WP:OABOT|OABOT]]''', a tool that finds open-access links for citations * '''[[m:Web2Cit|Web2Cit]]''': An automatic citation generator for web sources, meant to complement citation results by Citoid for which no valid ''translators'' exist. Web2Cit translators are community controlled. It runs [https://web2cit.toolforge.org/ its own server on toolforge]. * '''[https://www.mediawiki.org/wiki/Microsoft_Word_Macros#Wikipedia_AutoReferencer Wikipedia AutoReferencer]''', Microsoft word tool to convert embedded links to wikitext * '''[https://addons.mozilla.org/en/firefox/addon/wikipedia-references-creato/ Wikipedia-References-Creator]''': A Firefox addon for creation of references (output changeable). * [[User:Odie5533/SnipManager|SnipManager]] adds a ribbon interface to add citations * [[User:Cacycle/wikEd|wikEd]] * The [[Wikipedia:VisualEditor|visual editor]] helps users format, insert, and edit sources by simply providing a DOI, URL, ISBN etc., see [[WP:REFVISUAL]]. * The citation generation tool of the Visual Editor ([[WP:REFVISUAL]]) can also be used when editing the article source, for users who have enabled the [https://www.mediawiki.org/wiki/2017_wikitext_editor 2017 wikitext editor] in their preferences. * [[User:Badgettrg]], [https://sumsearch.org/cite/ Biomedical citation maker]. uses Pubmed ID (PMID) or DOI or PMCID or NCT. Adds links to [https://annals.org/aim/journal-club ACP Journal Club] and [https://ebm.bmj.com/ Evidence-Based Medicine] comments if present. * [[Template:Ref info]], which can aid evaluating what kind of citation style was used to write the article * Based on [[Wikipedia:The Wikipedia Library/Citoid|Citoid]]: ** [[Wikipedia:VisualEditor/User guide#Using standard cite templates|Cite templates in Visual Editor]] ** [[User:Salix alba/Citoid]] a client for the [[mw:citoid]] server which generates {{cs1}} templates from urls. * Hosted on tools.wmflabs.org: ** [[Wikipedia:refToolbar 2.0]], used in the Source Editor ** [[User:Citation bot|Citation bot]] ** [https://tools.wmflabs.org/yadkard/ Yadkard]: A web-based tool for generating [[Help:Shortened footnotes|shortened footnotes]] and citation using Google Books URLs, DOI or ISBN. Also supports some news websites. ** [https://tools.wmflabs.org/citation-template-filling/cgi-bin/index.cgi?ddb=&type=pubmed_id&id=&add_param_space=1&add_ref_tag=1&full_journal_title=1 Wikipedia template filling] – generates [[:en:Vancouver style|Vancouver style]] citations from PMIDs ([[:en:PubMed|PubMed]] IDs). ===Templates=== * [https://citations.toolforge.org/ Citation Bot] ([[User:Citation bot|more info]]) – Partial citations must either contain a DOI, PMID, PMC, ISBN, [[:en:S2CID|S2CID]] or enough fields to be uniquely found; the bot will also fix formatting errors * [[User:Rjwilmsi/CiteCompletion|CiteCompletion]] for [[WP:AWB|AutoWikiBrowser]] – A custom module for [[WP:AWB|AWB]] to complete citations of common English-language news sites * [[Template:Find sources|Find sources]] – provides links to customized searches for reliable sources in news newspapers books scholar JSTOR free images and the like. Points to a variety of templates for use in talk pages, on the [[Wikipedia:Articles for deletion|AfD]] page etc., and for a variety of types of articles and situations. * [[User:PleaseStand/References segregator|References segregator]] ([[:en:JavaScript|JavaScript]]) – Shows references in a separate edit window for easy editing; can also convert references to [[Help:Footnotes#List-defined references|list-defined references]] format * [[Wikipedia:RefToolbar|refToolbar]] ([[:en:JavaScript|JavaScript]]) – Allows you to format a reference during editing when you already have all the data * [[User:Smith609/Scopus|Scopus search add-on]] – Find a reference on [[:en:Scopus|Scopus]], then with one click it's formatted ready for use in an article * [[User:Odie5533/SnipManager|SnipManager]] ([[:en:JavaScript|JavaScript]]) – Adds a Ribbon menu above the edit form with templates (including citations) and the ability to preview citations * [[User:CitationTool]] – Semi-bot for finding citation errors and fixing them === Bookmarklets === * [[Wikipedia:Cite4Wiki|Cite4Wiki]], an [[:en:XUL|XUL]]-based add-on for [[:en:Pale Moon|Pale Moon]] to generate {{tl|cite web}} and {{tl|cite news}} for the browser's current page. * [[User:Bazzargh/citemark|citemark]] ([[:en:JavaScript|JavaScript]]) – A [[:en:Bookmarklet|Bookmarklet]] to help create {{tl|cite web}} templates; see the [[User:Bazzargh/citemark|developer's page]] for details * '''[[User:Ark25/RefScript|RefScript]]''': A bookmarklet that generates references with a single click. Works with a few news websites (BBC, Daily Mirror, Daily Telegraph, Huffington Post, Huffington Post Canada, New York Times, Washington Post, Boston Globe, Times of India, Financial Times, The Economist, Business Week, Ars Technica, TG Daily) and it can learn any other newspaper or website. * '''[[User:V111P/js/WebRef|WebRef]]''': A bookmarklet automating the filling of the {{tl|cite web}} template. * [[User:Ark25/RefScript]], a JavaScript bookmarklet – creates references in one click, works for many newspapers * [[User:V111P/js/WebRef]], a script or bookmarklet automating the filling of the {{tl|cite web}} template. You use the script on the page you want to cite. === Desktop software === * [[Wikipedia:Wikicite|Wikicite]] is a free program that helps editors to create citations for their Wikipedia contributions using [[Wikipedia:citation templates|citation templates]]. It is written in [[Visual Basic .NET]], making it suitable only for users with the [[:en:.NET Framework|.NET Framework]] installed on Windows, or, for other platforms, the [[:en:Mono (software)|Mono]] alternative framework. Wikicite and its source code is freely available; see the [[Wikipedia:Wikicite|developer's page]] for further details. ===Reference management software=== Reference management software can output formatted citations in several styles, including Wikipedia citation templates. * [[:en:Comparison of reference management software|Comparison of reference management software]] – side-by-side comparison of various [[:en:Reference management software|Reference management software]] * [[Wikipedia:Citing sources with Zotero]] – essay on using [[:en:Zotero|Zotero]] to quickly add citations to articles. * [[User:Nathanstrait/Wikipedia BibDesk Export Template|BibDesk Export Template]] – An export template for formatting [[Wikipedia:Citation templates|Wikipedia citation templates]] in [[:en:BibDesk|BibDesk]], which is a free [[:en:Open-source|Open-source]] [[:en:BibTeX|BibTeX]]-based [[:en:Reference management software|Reference management software]] application for [[:en:MacOS|MacOS]] ===User scripts=== * [[User:TheDJ/Folded references|Folded references]] collapses the references of a page * [[User:Blue-Haired Lawyer/Footnote popups|Footnote popups]] Displays a small popup box of a footnote when you hover the cursor above a footnote link * [[User:Ucucha/HarvErrors|HarvErrors]] shows errors when using Harvard templates * [[Template:Listref|ListrefErrors]] shows errors when using {{tl|listref}} * [[User:PleaseStand/References segregator|PleaseStand/References segregator]] places references into a separate edit box * [[User:Smith609/endnote.js|Reference exporter]] * [[User:Cumbril/Reference Organizer|Reference Organizer]] presents all references in graphical user interface, where you can choose whether the references should be defined in the body of article or in the reference list template(s) ([[Help:List-defined references|list-defined format]]). You can also sort the references in various ways (and optionally keep the sort order), and rename the references. * [[User:Anomie/reftooltip.js|RefTooltip]] sets the tooltip for references to the text of the reference. * [[User:Lingzhi2/reviewsourcecheck|ReviewSourceCheck]] (different versions for different preferences) flags 16 types of errors in the references and/or notes, when using Harv templates. * [[User:Ohconfucius/script/Sources|Sources]] Formats names of newspapers within citation templates * [[User:Anomie/ajaxpreview.js]] adds a preview button that will show references when editing a section * [[User:BrandonXLF/Autoref]] replaces the reference button in the editing toolbar in the 2010 wikitext editor with a button that allows the use of Citoid to insert an auto generated reference * [[User:BrandonXLF/Citoid]] generates a reference using the Citoid server. Designed for being used inside user scripts. * [[user:js/ajaxPreview]] adds a preview button that will show references when editing a section * [[User:Salix alba/Citoid]] Generates citation templates using the Citoid server. Standalone javascript which can be used outside of Visual Editor. == Tools to improve existing citations == * [[Wikipedia:Citation expander|Citation expander]] automatically adds missing data to citations using citation templates, and makes corrections to their formatting * [[Wikipedia:ReFill|reFill]] expands bare url references ==Duplicate reference finders== Finding duplicate references by examining reference lists is difficult. There are some tools that can help: * [[Wikipedia:AutoWikiBrowser|AutoWikiBrowser]] (AWB) will identify and (usually) correct ''exact duplicates'' between <ref>...</ref> tags. See [[Wikipedia:HTADC|the documentation]]. * [https://www.convertcsv.com/url-extractor.htm URL Extractor For Web Pages and Text] can identify Web citations with the ''exact same URL'' but otherwise possibly different. Most differences are not significant, but sometimes different page numbers from the same URL are cited. Occasionally references to the same Web page might be followed by different non-significant tracking parameters (?utm ..., #ixzz...), and will not be listed as duplicates. ** Step 1: click "Enter URL", enter (paste) the URL of the Wikipedia article and click "Load", ** Step 2: tick "Only Display duplicate URL addresses" (which unticks "Remove duplicate addresses") ***Optional: Tick the radio button "Do not show", tick the box at the beginning of its line, and enter (paste) into the box web.archive.org,wikipedia,wikimedia,wikiquote,wikidata,worldcat ** Step 3: Click Extract. ** Then the duplicates will be listed, and must be manually merged. There will often be false positives; web.archive.org URLs, in particular, are a nuisance as they contain the original URLs, which show as duplicates. The optional part of Step 2 eliminates the archive URLs, but unfortunately the list of duplicates includes the archived pages. The wiki* URLs are less of a problem as they can just be ignored. == Libraries for developers == * [[User:Richiez]] wrote {{oldid2|536234043|Scripts to do automatic PMID/ISBN referencing|tools to automatically handle citations}} for a whole article at a time. These were marked as [[Special:Diff/705834247|obsolete in 2016]]. They convert occurrences of {{pmid|XXXXXXXX}} ([[:en:PubMed|PubMed]]) or {{isbn|XXXXXXXXXX}} to properly formatted footnote or Harvard-style references. Written in [[:en:Ruby (programming language)|Ruby]] and requires a working installation with basic libraries. * [https://github.com/lindenb/xslt-sandbox/blob/master/stylesheets/bio/ncbi/pubmed2wikipedia.xsl pubmed2wikipedia.xsl] an [[:en:Extensible Stylesheet Language|XSL]] stylesheet transforming the XML output of [[:en:PubMed|PubMed]] to Wikipedia refs. ==Documentation== These templates can be used in documentation: '''Typing aids''' * {{tl|cite *}}: shortcut for {{tl|Citation/core}} * {{tl|cite xxx}}: shortcut for [[Wikipedia:Citation templates]] * {{tl|cite.php}}: shortcut for [[mw:Extension:Cite]] * {{tl|cs1}}: shortcut for [[Help: Citation Style 1]] * {{tl|cs2}}: shortcut for [[Help: Citation Style 2]] * {{tl|fnote}}: shortcut for [[Help:Footnotes]] * {{tl|ldr}}: shortcut for [[Help:Footnotes#WP:LDR|List-defined references]] * {{tl|paren}}: shortcut for [[Wikipedia:Parenthetical referencing]] * {{tl|sfnote}}: shortcut for [[Help:Shortened footnotes]] * {{tl|vcite}}: shortcut for [[Help:Citation Style Vancouver]] '''Navboxes''' * {{tl|Citation Style 1}}: navbox for {{cs1}} templates * {{tl|Wikipedia referencing}}: navbox for referencing help pages '''Debug''' * {{tl|Cite compare}}: a tool for comparing the output of the various versions of a {{cs1}} template '''Documentation''' * {{tl|Citation Style documentation}}: modular documentation for {{cs1}} and {{cs2}} * {{tl|Harvard citation documentation}}: documentation for Harvard citations * {{tl|Markup}}: show markup and rendered output side by side * {{tl|Refname rules}}: rules for names for footnotes and groups '''Replication''' * {{tl|Dummy backlink}}: replicate footnote backlinks * {{tl|Dummy ref}}: replicate footnote in-text cite * {{tl|Fake heading}}: replicate headings * {{tl|Fake notes and references}}: replicate Notes and References headings {{Wikipedia referencing}} {{Wikipedia technical help|collapsed}} [[Category:Wikipedia tools]]

The optional part of Step 2 eliminates the archive URLs, but unfortunately the list of duplicates includes the archived pages. The wiki* URLs are less of a problem as they can just be ignored. == Libraries for developers == * [[User:Richiez]] wrote {{oldid2|536234043|Scripts to do automatic PMID/ISBN referencing|tools to automatically handle citations}} for a whole article at a time. These were marked as [[Special:Diff/705834247|obsolete in 2016]]. They convert occurrences of {{pmid|XXXXXXXX}} ([[:en:PubMed|PubMed]]) or {{isbn|XXXXXXXXXX}} to properly formatted footnote or Harvard-style references. Written in [[:en:Ruby (programming language)|Ruby]] and requires a working installation with basic libraries. * [https://github.com/lindenb/xslt-sandbox/blob/master/stylesheets/bio/ncbi/pubmed2wikipedia.xsl pubmed2wikipedia.xsl] an [[:en:Extensible Stylesheet Language|XSL]] stylesheet transforming the XML output of [[:en:PubMed|PubMed]] to Wikipedia refs. ==Documentation== These templates can be used in documentation: '''Typing aids''' * {{tl|cite *}}: shortcut for {{tl|Citation/core}} * {{tl|cite xxx}}: shortcut for [[Wikipedia:Citation templates]] * {{tl|cite.php}}: shortcut for [[mw:Extension:Cite]] * {{tl|cs1}}: shortcut for [[Help: Citation Style 1]] * {{tl|cs2}}: shortcut for [[Help: Citation Style 2]] * {{tl|fnote}}: shortcut for [[Help:Footnotes]] * {{tl|ldr}}: shortcut for [[Help:Footnotes#WP:LDR|List-defined references]] * {{tl|paren}}: shortcut for [[Wikipedia:Parenthetical referencing]] * {{tl|sfnote}}: shortcut for [[Help:Shortened footnotes]] * {{tl|vcite}}: shortcut for [[Help:Citation Style Vancouver]] '''Navboxes''' * {{tl|Citation Style 1}}: navbox for {{cs1}} templates * {{tl|Wikipedia referencing}}: navbox for referencing help pages '''Debug''' * {{tl|Cite compare}}: a tool for comparing the output of the various versions of a {{cs1}} template '''Documentation''' * {{tl|Citation Style documentation}}: modular documentation for {{cs1}} and {{cs2}} * {{tl|Harvard citation documentation}}: documentation for Harvard citations * {{tl|Markup}}: show markup and rendered output side by side * {{tl|Refname rules}}: rules for names for footnotes and groups '''Replication''' * {{tl|Dummy backlink}}: replicate footnote backlinks * {{tl|Dummy ref}}: replicate footnote in-text cite * {{tl|Fake heading}}: replicate headings * {{tl|Fake notes and references}}: replicate Notes and References headings {{Wikipedia referencing}} {{Wikipedia technical help|collapsed}} [[Category:Wikipedia tools]]

revision.text: {{Short description|Wikimedia project page for citation}} {{pp-semi-indef|small=yes}} {{redirect|Wikipedia:Ref tools|referencing toolbar that is part of the default Wikipedia editor|Wikipedia:RefToolbar}} {{Wikipedia how to|WP:CITETOOL}} The following tools can help you assemble a citation from limited information, with limited effort, although you should check their output for accuracy. These are tools with a variety of interfaces that provide a complete formatted reference based on a few initial details. == General == * [[User:ProveIt GT|ProveIt]] provides a graphical interface for editing, adding, and citing references. It may be enabled per the documentation * [[Wikipedia:RefToolbar|RefToolbar]] is part of the editing tool bar == Generators == {{shortcut|WP:CITEGENERATORS}} {{See also|Wikipedia:Citing sources#Programming tools}} {{Editor tools}} * '''[http://sumsearch.org/cite/ Biomedical citation maker]''' by [[User:Badgettrg]]. Converts a PubMed ID (PMID), DOI, PMCID, NCT to {{tl|cite journal}} or simple wikiformatting for all journals indexed by PubMed. Bookmarklet is available. Adds links to [https://web.archive.org/web/20161022143433/http://annals.org/aim/journal-club ACP Journal Club] and [http://ebm.bmj.com/ Evidence-Based Medicine] comments if present. *'''[https://citationhunt.toolforge.org/ Citation Hunt]''': A tool for browsing snippets of Wikipedia articles that [[Template:cn|lack citations]]. * '''[https://citer.toolforge.org/ Citer]''': Converts a URL, DOI, ISBN, PMID, PMCID, [[:en:OCLC|OCLC]], or [[:en:Google Books|Google Books]] URL into a citation and [[Help:Shortened footnotes|shortened footnote]]. It also can generate citations for [https://github.com/5j9/citer#what-does-it-do certain major news websites] (e.g., [[:en:The New York Times|The New York Times]]) and the [[:en:Wayback Machine|Wayback Machine]]. * '''[[Wikipedia:TWL/Citoid|Citoid]]''': A tool built into both [[wikipedia:VisualEditor|Visual Editor]] and source editor that attempts to build a full citation based on a URL. See [[Wikipedia:VisualEditor/User guide#Using Citoid|user guide]]. * '''[https://citation-template-filling.toolforge.org/cgi-bin/index.cgi Diberri Template builder]''': Converts URL, DrugBank ID, HGNC ID, PubMed ID, PubMed Central ID or PubChem ID to full citation. * '''[https://makeref.toolforge.org MakeRef]''': A form for creating various {{cite xxx}} templates. * '''[[WP:OABOT|OABOT]]''', a tool that finds open-access links for citations * '''[[m:Web2Cit|Web2Cit]]''': An automatic citation generator for web sources, meant to complement citation results by Citoid for which no valid ''translators'' exist. Web2Cit translators are community controlled. It runs [https://web2cit.toolforge.org/ its own server on toolforge]. * '''[https://www.mediawiki.org/wiki/Microsoft_Word_Macros#Wikipedia_AutoReferencer Wikipedia AutoReferencer]''', Microsoft word tool to convert embedded links to wikitext * '''[https://addons.mozilla.org/en/firefox/addon/wikipedia-references-creato/ Wikipedia-References-Creator]''': A Firefox addon for creation of references (output changeable). * [[User:Odie5533/SnipManager|SnipManager]] adds a ribbon interface to add citations * [[User:Cacycle/wikEd|wikEd]] * The [[Wikipedia:VisualEditor|visual editor]] helps users format, insert, and edit sources by simply providing a DOI, URL, ISBN etc., see [[WP:REFVISUAL]]. * The citation generation tool of the Visual Editor ([[WP:REFVISUAL]]) can also be used when editing the article source, for users who have enabled the [https://www.mediawiki.org/wiki/2017_wikitext_editor 2017 wikitext editor] in their preferences. * [[User:Badgettrg]], [https://sumsearch.org/cite/ Biomedical citation maker]. uses Pubmed ID (PMID) or DOI or PMCID or NCT. Adds links to [https://annals.org/aim/journal-club ACP Journal Club] and [https://ebm.bmj.com/ Evidence-Based Medicine] comments if present. * [[Template:Ref info]], which can aid evaluating what kind of citation style was used to write the article * Based on [[Wikipedia:The Wikipedia Library/Citoid|Citoid]]: ** [[Wikipedia:VisualEditor/User guide#Using standard cite templates|Cite templates in Visual Editor]] ** [[User:Salix alba/Citoid]] a client for the [[mw:citoid]] server which generates {{cs1}} templates from urls. * Hosted on tools.wmflabs.org: ** [[Wikipedia:refToolbar 2.0]], used in the Source Editor ** [[User:Citation bot|Citation bot]] ** [https://tools.wmflabs.org/yadkard/ Yadkard]: A web-based tool for generating [[Help:Shortened footnotes|shortened footnotes]] and citation using Google Books URLs, DOI or ISBN. Also supports some news websites. ** [https://tools.wmflabs.org/citation-template-filling/cgi-bin/index.cgi?ddb=&type=pubmed_id&id=&add_param_space=1&add_ref_tag=1&full_journal_title=1 Wikipedia template filling] – generates [[:en:Vancouver style|Vancouver style]] citations from PMIDs ([[:en:PubMed|PubMed]] IDs). ===Templates=== * [https://citations.toolforge.org/ Citation Bot] ([[User:Citation bot|more info]]) – Partial citations must either contain a DOI, PMID, PMC, ISBN, [[:en:S2CID|S2CID]] or enough fields to be uniquely found; the bot will also fix formatting errors * [[User:Rjwilmsi/CiteCompletion|CiteCompletion]] for [[WP:AWB|AutoWikiBrowser]] – A custom module for [[WP:AWB|AWB]] to complete citations of common English-language news sites * [[Template:Find sources|Find sources]] – provides links to customized searches for reliable sources in news newspapers books scholar JSTOR free images and the like. Points to a variety of templates for use in talk pages, on the [[Wikipedia:Articles for deletion|AfD]] page etc., and for a variety of types of articles and situations. * [[User:PleaseStand/References segregator|References segregator]] ([[:en:JavaScript|JavaScript]]) – Shows references in a separate edit window for easy editing; can also convert references to [[Help:Footnotes#List-defined references|list-defined references]] format * [[Wikipedia:RefToolbar|refToolbar]] ([[:en:JavaScript|JavaScript]]) – Allows you to format a reference during editing when you already have all the data * [[User:Smith609/Scopus|Scopus search add-on]] – Find a reference on [[:en:Scopus|Scopus]], then with one click it's formatted ready for use in an article * [[User:Odie5533/SnipManager|SnipManager]] ([[:en:JavaScript|JavaScript]]) – Adds a Ribbon menu above the edit form with templates (including citations) and the ability to preview citations * [[User:CitationTool]] – Semi-bot for finding citation errors and fixing them === Bookmarklets === * [[Wikipedia:Cite4Wiki|Cite4Wiki]], an [[:en:XUL|XUL]]-based add-on for [[:en:Pale Moon|Pale Moon]] to generate {{tl|cite web}} and {{tl|cite news}} for the browser's current page. * [[User:Bazzargh/citemark|citemark]] ([[:en:JavaScript|JavaScript]]) – A [[:en:Bookmarklet|Bookmarklet]] to help create {{tl|cite web}} templates; see the [[User:Bazzargh/citemark|developer's page]] for details * '''[[User:Ark25/RefScript|RefScript]]''': A bookmarklet that generates references with a single click. Works with a few news websites (BBC, Daily Mirror, Daily Telegraph, Huffington Post, Huffington Post Canada, New York Times, Washington Post, Boston Globe, Times of India, Financial Times, The Economist, Business Week, Ars Technica, TG Daily) and it can learn any other newspaper or website. * '''[[User:V111P/js/WebRef|WebRef]]''': A bookmarklet automating the filling of the {{tl|cite web}} template. * [[User:Ark25/RefScript]], a JavaScript bookmarklet – creates references in one click, works for many newspapers * [[User:V111P/js/WebRef]], a script or bookmarklet automating the filling of the {{tl|cite web}} template. You use the script on the page you want to cite. === Desktop software === * [[Wikipedia:Wikicite|Wikicite]] is a free program that helps editors to create citations for their Wikipedia contributions using [[Wikipedia:citation templates|citation templates]]. It is written in [[:en:Visual Basic .NET|Visual Basic .NET]], making it suitable only for users with the [[:en:.NET Framework|.NET Framework]] installed on Windows, or, for other platforms, the [[:en:Mono (software)|Mono]] alternative framework. Wikicite and its source code is freely available; see the [[Wikipedia:Wikicite|developer's page]] for further details. ===Reference management software=== Reference management software can output formatted citations in several styles, including Wikipedia citation templates. * [[:en:Comparison of reference management software|Comparison of reference management software]] – side-by-side comparison of various [[:en:Reference management software|Reference management software]] * [[Wikipedia:Citing sources with Zotero]] – essay on using [[:en:Zotero|Zotero]] to quickly add citations to articles. * [[User:Nathanstrait/Wikipedia BibDesk Export Template|BibDesk Export Template]] – An export template for formatting [[Wikipedia:Citation templates|Wikipedia citation templates]] in [[:en:BibDesk|BibDesk]], which is a free [[:en:Open-source|Open-source]] [[:en:BibTeX|BibTeX]]-based [[:en:Reference management software|Reference management software]] application for [[:en:MacOS|MacOS]] ===User scripts=== * [[User:TheDJ/Folded references|Folded references]] collapses the references of a page * [[User:Blue-Haired Lawyer/Footnote popups|Footnote popups]] Displays a small popup box of a footnote when you hover the cursor above a footnote link * [[User:Ucucha/HarvErrors|HarvErrors]] shows errors when using Harvard templates * [[Template:Listref|ListrefErrors]] shows errors when using {{tl|listref}} * [[User:PleaseStand/References segregator|PleaseStand/References segregator]] places references into a separate edit box * [[User:Smith609/endnote.js|Reference exporter]] * [[User:Cumbril/Reference Organizer|Reference Organizer]] presents all references in graphical user interface, where you can choose whether the references should be defined in the body of article or in the reference list template(s) ([[Help:List-defined references|list-defined format]]). You can also sort the references in various ways (and optionally keep the sort order), and rename the references. * [[User:Anomie/reftooltip.js|RefTooltip]] sets the tooltip for references to the text of the reference. * [[User:Lingzhi2/reviewsourcecheck|ReviewSourceCheck]] (different versions for different preferences) flags 16 types of errors in the references and/or notes, when using Harv templates. * [[User:Ohconfucius/script/Sources|Sources]] Formats names of newspapers within citation templates * [[User:Anomie/ajaxpreview.js]] adds a preview button that will show references when editing a section * [[User:BrandonXLF/Autoref]] replaces the reference button in the editing toolbar in the 2010 wikitext editor with a button that allows the use of Citoid to insert an auto generated reference * [[User:BrandonXLF/Citoid]] generates a reference using the Citoid server. Designed for being used inside user scripts. * [[user:js/ajaxPreview]] adds a preview button that will show references when editing a section * [[User:Salix alba/Citoid]] Generates citation templates using the Citoid server. Standalone javascript which can be used outside of Visual Editor. == Tools to improve existing citations == * [[Wikipedia:Citation expander|Citation expander]] automatically adds missing data to citations using citation templates, and makes corrections to their formatting * [[Wikipedia:ReFill|reFill]] expands bare url references ==Duplicate reference finders== Finding duplicate references by examining reference lists is difficult. There are some tools that can help: * [[Wikipedia:AutoWikiBrowser|AutoWikiBrowser]] (AWB) will identify and (usually) correct ''exact duplicates'' between <ref>...</ref> tags. See [[Wikipedia:HTADC|the documentation]]. * [https://www.convertcsv.com/url-extractor.htm URL Extractor For Web Pages and Text] can identify Web citations with the ''exact same URL'' but otherwise possibly different. Most differences are not significant, but sometimes different page numbers from the same URL are cited. Occasionally references to the same Web page might be followed by different non-significant tracking parameters (?utm ..., #ixzz...), and will not be listed as duplicates. ** Step 1: click "Enter URL", enter (paste) the URL of the Wikipedia article and click "Load", ** Step 2: tick "Only Display duplicate URL addresses" (which unticks "Remove duplicate addresses") ***Optional: Tick the radio button "Do not show", tick the box at the beginning of its line, and enter (paste) into the box web.archive.org,wikipedia,wikimedia,wikiquote,wikidata,worldcat ** Step 3: Click Extract. ** Then the duplicates will be listed, and must be manually merged. There will often be false positives; web.archive.org URLs, in particular, are a nuisance as they contain the original URLs, which show as duplicates. The optional part of Step 2 eliminates the archive URLs, but unfortunately the list of duplicates includes the archived pages. The wiki* URLs are less of a problem as they can just be ignored. == Libraries for developers == * [[User:Richiez]] wrote {{oldid2|536234043|Scripts to do automatic PMID/ISBN referencing|tools to automatically handle citations}} for a whole article at a time. These were marked as [[Special:Diff/705834247|obsolete in 2016]]. They convert occurrences of {{pmid|XXXXXXXX}} ([[:en:PubMed|PubMed]]) or {{isbn|XXXXXXXXXX}} to properly formatted footnote or Harvard-style references. Written in [[:en:Ruby (programming language)|Ruby]] and requires a working installation with basic libraries. * [https://github.com/lindenb/xslt-sandbox/blob/master/stylesheets/bio/ncbi/pubmed2wikipedia.xsl pubmed2wikipedia.xsl] an [[:en:Extensible Stylesheet Language|XSL]] stylesheet transforming the XML output of [[:en:PubMed|PubMed]] to Wikipedia refs. ==Documentation== These templates can be used in documentation: '''Typing aids''' * {{tl|cite *}}: shortcut for {{tl|Citation/core}} * {{tl|cite xxx}}: shortcut for [[Wikipedia:Citation templates]] * {{tl|cite.php}}: shortcut for [[mw:Extension:Cite]] * {{tl|cs1}}: shortcut for [[Help: Citation Style 1]] * {{tl|cs2}}: shortcut for [[Help: Citation Style 2]] * {{tl|fnote}}: shortcut for [[Help:Footnotes]] * {{tl|ldr}}: shortcut for [[Help:Footnotes#WP:LDR|List-defined references]] * {{tl|paren}}: shortcut for [[Wikipedia:Parenthetical referencing]] * {{tl|sfnote}}: shortcut for [[Help:Shortened footnotes]] * {{tl|vcite}}: shortcut for [[Help:Citation Style Vancouver]] '''Navboxes''' * {{tl|Citation Style 1}}: navbox for {{cs1}} templates * {{tl|Wikipedia referencing}}: navbox for referencing help pages '''Debug''' * {{tl|Cite compare}}: a tool for comparing the output of the various versions of a {{cs1}} template '''Documentation''' * {{tl|Citation Style documentation}}: modular documentation for {{cs1}} and {{cs2}} * {{tl|Harvard citation documentation}}: documentation for Harvard citations * {{tl|Markup}}: show markup and rendered output side by side * {{tl|Refname rules}}: rules for names for footnotes and groups '''Replication''' * {{tl|Dummy backlink}}: replicate footnote backlinks * {{tl|Dummy ref}}: replicate footnote in-text cite * {{tl|Fake heading}}: replicate headings * {{tl|Fake notes and references}}: replicate Notes and References headings {{Wikipedia referencing}} {{Wikipedia technical help|collapsed}} [[Category:Wikipedia tools]]

Web2Cit translators are community controlled. the bot will also fix formatting errors * [[User:Rjwilmsi/CiteCompletion|CiteCompletion]] for [[WP:AWB|AutoWikiBrowser]] – A custom module for [[WP:AWB|AWB]] to complete citations of common English-language news sites * [[Template:Find sources|Find sources]] – provides links to customized searches for reliable sources in news newspapers books scholar JSTOR free images and the like. Points to a variety of templates for use in talk pages, on the [[Wikipedia:Articles for deletion|AfD]] page etc., and for a variety of types of articles and situations. * [[User:PleaseStand/References segregator|References segregator]] ([[JavaScript]]) – Shows references in a separate edit window for easy editing; can also convert references to [[Help:Footnotes#List-defined references|list-defined references]] format * [[Wikipedia:RefToolbar|refToolbar]] ([[JavaScript]]) – Allows you to format a reference during editing when you already have all the data * [[User:Smith609/Scopus|Scopus search add-on]] – Find a reference on [[Scopus]], then with one click it's formatted ready for use in an article * [[User:Odie5533/SnipManager|SnipManager]] ([[JavaScript]]) – Adds a Ribbon menu above the edit form with templates (including citations) and the ability to preview citations * [[User:CitationTool]] – Semi-bot for finding citation errors and fixing them === Bookmarklets === * [[Wikipedia:Cite4Wiki|Cite4Wiki]], an [[XUL]]-based add-on for [[Pale Moon]] to generate {{tl|cite web}} and {{tl|cite news}} for the browser's current page. * [[User:Bazzargh/citemark|citemark]] ([[JavaScript]]) – A [[bookmarklet]] to help create {{tl|cite web}} templates; see the [[User:Bazzargh/citemark|developer's page]] for details * '''[[User:Ark25/RefScript|RefScript]]''': A bookmarklet that generates references with a single click. You use the script on the page you want to cite. === Desktop software === * [[Wikipedia:Wikicite|Wikicite]] is a free program that helps editors to create citations for their Wikipedia contributions using [[Wikipedia:citation templates|citation templates]]. It is written in [[Visual Basic .NET]], making it suitable only for users with the [[.NET Framework]] installed on Windows, or, for other platforms, the [[Mono (software)|Mono]] alternative framework. Wikicite and its source code is freely available; see the [[Wikipedia:Wikicite|developer's page]] for further details. ===Reference management software=== Reference management software can output formatted citations in several styles, including Wikipedia citation templates. * [[Comparison of reference management software]] – side-by-side comparison of various [[reference management software]] * [[Wikipedia:Citing sources with Zotero]] – essay on using [[Zotero]] to quickly add citations to articles. * [[User:Nathanstrait/Wikipedia BibDesk Export Template|BibDesk Export Template]] – An export template for formatting [[Wikipedia:Citation templates|Wikipedia citation templates]] in [[BibDesk]], which is a free [[open-source]] [[BibTeX]]-based [[reference management software]] application for [[macOS]] ===User scripts=== * [[User:TheDJ/Folded references|Folded references]] collapses the references of a page * [[User:Blue-Haired Lawyer/Footnote popups|Footnote popups]] Displays a small popup box of a footnote when you hover the cursor above a footnote link * [[User:Ucucha/HarvErrors|HarvErrors]] shows errors when using Harvard templates * [[Template:Listref|ListrefErrors]] shows errors when using {{tl|listref}} * [[User:PleaseStand/References segregator|PleaseStand/References segregator]] places references into a separate edit box * [[User:Smith609/endnote.js|Reference exporter]] * [[User:Cumbril/Reference Organizer|Reference Organizer]] presents all references in graphical user interface, where you can choose whether the references should be defined in the body of article or in the reference list template(s) ([[Help:List-defined references|list-defined format]]). You can also sort the references in various ways (and optionally keep the sort order), and rename the references. * [[User:Anomie/reftooltip.js|RefTooltip]] sets the tooltip for references to the text of the reference. * [[User:Lingzhi2/reviewsourcecheck|ReviewSourceCheck]] (different versions for different preferences) flags 16 types of errors in the references and/or notes, when using Harv templates. * [[User:Ohconfucius/script/Sources|Sources]] Formats names of newspapers within citation templates * [[User:Anomie/ajaxpreview.js]] adds a preview button that will show references when editing a section * [[User:BrandonXLF/Autoref]] replaces the reference button in the editing toolbar in the 2010 wikitext editor with a button that allows the use of Citoid to insert an auto generated reference * [[User:BrandonXLF/Citoid]] generates a reference using the Citoid server. Designed for being used inside user scripts. * [[user:js/ajaxPreview]] adds a preview button that will show references when editing a section * [[User:Salix alba/Citoid]] Generates citation templates using the Citoid server. Standalone javascript which can be used outside of Visual Editor. == Tools to improve existing citations == * [[Wikipedia:Citation expander|Citation expander]] automatically adds missing data to citations using citation templates, and makes corrections to their formatting * [[Wikipedia:ReFill|reFill]] expands bare url references ===Citation verification=== * [[User:Alaexis/AI Source Verification]] checks whether a source supports the claim it's attached to. Works for sources that are available online. ==Duplicate reference finders== Finding duplicate references by examining reference lists is difficult. There are some tools that can help: * [[Wikipedia:AutoWikiBrowser|AutoWikiBrowser]] (AWB) will identify and (usually) correct ''exact duplicates'' between <ref>...</ref> tags. See [[Wikipedia:HTADC|the documentation]]. * [[User:Polygnotus/DuplicateReferences]] This script detects and displays references with duplicate URLs. It also calculates how similar the visible text of those references is as a percentage. * [https://www.convertcsv.com/url-extractor.htm URL Extractor For Web Pages and Text] can identify Web citations with the ''exact same URL'' but otherwise possibly different. Most differences are not significant, but sometimes different page numbers from the same URL are cited. Occasionally references to the same Web page might be followed by different non-significant tracking parameters (?utm ..., #ixzz...), and will not be listed as duplicates. ** Step 1: click "Enter URL", enter (paste) the URL of the Wikipedia article and click "Load", ** Step 2: tick "Only Display duplicate URL addresses" (which unticks "Remove duplicate addresses") ***Optional: Tick the radio button "Do not show", tick the box at the beginning of its line, and enter (paste) into the box web.archive.org,wikipedia,wikimedia,wikiquote,wikidata,worldcat ** Step 3: Click Extract. ** Then the duplicates will be listed, and must be manually merged. There will often be false positives; web.archive.org URLs, in particular, are a nuisance as they contain the original URLs, which show as duplicates. The optional part of Step 2 eliminates the archive URLs, but unfortunately the list of duplicates includes the archived pages. The wiki* URLs are less of a problem as they can just be ignored. == Libraries for developers == * [[User:Richiez]] wrote {{oldid2|536234043|Scripts to do automatic PMID/ISBN referencing|tools to automatically handle citations}} for a whole article at a time. These were marked as [[Special:Diff/705834247|obsolete in 2016]]. They convert occurrences of {{pmid|XXXXXXXX}} ([[PubMed]]) or {{isbn|XXXXXXXXXX}} to properly formatted footnote or Harvard-style references. Written in [[Ruby (programming language)|Ruby]] and requires a working installation with basic libraries. * [https://github.com/lindenb/xslt-sandbox/blob/master/stylesheets/bio/ncbi/pubmed2wikipedia.xsl pubmed2wikipedia.xsl] an [[Extensible Stylesheet Language|XSL]] stylesheet transforming the XML output of [[PubMed]] to Wikipedia refs. ==Documentation== These templates can be used in documentation: '''Typing aids''' * {{tl|cite *}}: shortcut for {{tl|Citation/core}} * {{tl|cite xxx}}: shortcut for [[Wikipedia:Citation templates]] * {{tl|cite.php}}: shortcut for [[mw:Extension:Cite]] * {{tl|cs1}}: shortcut for [[Help: Citation Style 1]] * {{tl|cs2}}: shortcut for [[Help: Citation Style 2]] * {{tl|fnote}}: shortcut for [[Help:Footnotes]] * {{tl|ldr}}: shortcut for [[Help:Footnotes#WP:LDR|List-defined references]] * {{tl|paren}}: shortcut for [[Wikipedia:Parenthetical referencing]] * {{tl|sfnote}}: shortcut for [[Help:Shortened footnotes]] '''Navboxes''' * {{tl|Citation Style 1}}: navbox for {{cs1}} templates * {{tl|Wikipedia referencing}}: navbox for referencing help pages '''Debug''' * {{tl|Cite compare}}: a tool for comparing the output of the various versions of a {{cs1}} template '''Documentation''' * {{tl|Citation Style documentation}}: modular documentation for {{cs1}} and {{cs2}} * {{tl|Harvard citation documentation}}: documentation for Harvard citations * {{tl|Markup}}: show markup and rendered output side by side * {{tl|Refname rules}}: rules for names for footnotes and groups '''Replication''' * {{tl|Dummy backlink}}: replicate footnote backlinks * {{tl|Dummy ref}}: replicate footnote in-text cite * {{tl|Fake heading}}: replicate headings * {{tl|Fake notes and references}}: replicate Notes and References headings {{Wikipedia referencing}} {{Wikipedia technical help|collapsed}} {{Citation metadata navbox}} {{Authority control}} [[Category:Wikipedia tools]] [[Category:Wikipedia citation/link tools| ]]
